zh.Smallburgh, Norfolk, England, United Kin: 英國英格蘭諾福爾可斯馬爾包
zh.lifeSummary: 當Walter de Ingham於大約1200年出生在英國諾福爾可英厄姆時，他的父親Sir John d'Ingham26歲，母親Albreda de Waleran22歲。他和Mrs. Alice de Ingham至少有4個兒子。他去世於大約1253年在英國諾福爾可希可林享年55歲。

zh-hans.Smallburgh, Norfolk, England, United Kin: 英国英格兰諾福爾可斯馬爾包
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Walter de Ingham于大约1200年出生在英国諾福爾可英厄姆时，他的父亲Sir John d'Ingham26岁，母亲Albreda de Waleran22岁。他和Mrs. Alice de Ingham至少有4个儿子。他去世于大约1253年在英国諾福爾可希可林享年55岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Ingham
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): habitational name from any of the places called Ingham, particularly in Lincolnshire, Norfolk, and Suffolk. The placename is from the Old English personal name Inga + hām ‘homestead’. In Yorkshire this name was sometimes confused with Bingham in the 18th century.
English (of Norman origin): variant of the obsolete Norman surname Engaine; see Gain .

variants.name: Bingham
meanings: English (Dorset) and Irish (County Mayo): habitational name from Bingham (Nottinghamshire). The placename is probably from an Old English folk-name Bynningas (‘the people associated with a man named Bynna’), or possibly from an unattested Old English word bing ‘a kettle-shaped hollow’, + Old English hām ‘homestead’.
Irish (Ulster, of Scottish origin): altered form of   Bigham  .
American shortened and altered form of various like-sounding Jewish surnames such as   Bingenheimer  .

variants.name: Gain
meanings: English and Scottish (of Norman origin): nickname for a cunning person, from Engaine, which is based on Old French engaigne ‘ingenuity, cunning, trickery’.

English: habitiational name from any of various places called after the Engain family, including Colne Engaine and Gaynes Park (Essex), d'Engaine's Farm (Cambridgeshire), and Aston Ingham (Hereford). Engaine (see 1 above) was the name of a powerful Norman family with lands in several counties. The original form of the surname has since died out, surviving as Gain and its variants. There has also been considerable confusion with Ingham .
French: nickname from a noun derivative of Old French gaainier ‘to obtain, gain, win’.

nameMeanings.name: Walter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French personal name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from wald ‘rule’ + heri, hari ‘army’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, superseding the native Old English form, Wealdhere. It was a very popular name in medieval England, normally pronounced ‘Water’.

variants.name: Walther
meanings: from wald ‘rule’ + heri ‘army’; name of the famous medieval minnesinger Walther von der Vogelweide ( c.1170– c.1230 ).

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When Walter de Ingham was born about 1200, in Ingham, Norfolk, England, his father, Sir John d'Ingham, was 26 and his mother, Albreda de Waleran, was 22. He had at least 4 sons with Mrs. Alice de Ingham. He died about 1253, in Hickling, Norfolk, England, at the age of 55.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Walter de Ingham est né environ 1200, à Ingham, Norfolk, Angleterre, son père, Sir John d'Ingham, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Albreda de Waleran, avait 22 ans. Il a eu au moins 4 fils avec Mrs. Alice de Ingham. Il est décédé environ 1253, à Hickling, Norfolk, Angleterre, à l’âge de 55 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Walter de Ingham um 1200 in Ingham, Norfolk, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir John d'Ingham 26 und seine Mutter Albreda de Waleran 22. Er hatte mit Mrs. Alice de Ingham mindestens 4 Söhne. Er starb um 1253 im Alter von 55 Jahren in Hickling, Norfolk, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Walter de Ingham è nato il circa 1200 nel Ingham, Norfolk, England, il suo padre, Sir John d'Ingham, aveva 26 anni e il suo madre, Albreda de Waleran, aveva 22 anni. Lui avuto almeno 4 figli con Mrs. Alice de Ingham. Morì circa 1253, nel Hickling, Norfolk, England, all'età di 55 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Walter de Ingham(이)가 대략 1200년 영국 노르폴크 잉함에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir John d'Ingham(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Albreda de Waleran(은)는 22세였다. 그는 Mrs. Alice de Ingham(와)과 적어도 네 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 대략 1253년 영국 노르폴크 힉링에서 55세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Walter de Ingham nasceu aproximadamente 1200, em Ingham, Norfolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir John d'Ingham, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Albreda de Waleran, tinha 22 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 4 filhos com Mrs. Alice de Ingham. Ele faleceu aproximadamente 1253, em Hickling, Norfolk, Inglaterra, com 55 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Walter de Ingham родился около 1200 г. в местности ингхам, норфолк, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir John d'Ingham было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Albreda de Waleran, 22. У него было по крайней мере 4 сына от Mrs. Alice de Ingham. Он умер около 1253 г. в местности хикклинг, норфолк, Англия в возрасте 55 лет.

Tuvo por lo menos 4 hijos con Mrs. Alice de Ingham. Murió aproximadamente 1253, en Hickling, Norfolk, Inglaterra, a la edad de 55 años.
